rationale.root_cause: They correctly point to program.c:9 (strcpy to buf from argv[1]) and explain argv input can exceed the buffer, but they do not explicitly name the resulting stack_buffer_overflow as required.
rationale.local_fix: They switch to strncpy and ensure null-termination, but they do not reject overlong input before copying.
rationale.global_fix: They propose replacing unsafe string APIs (strcpy, strcat, etc.) with bounded equivalents across the codebase, which removes raw, unbounded string handling from the data path.
